draft.introduction: John 4:7–42 presents the encounter between Jesus and the Samaritan woman at Jacob’s well. The dialogue moves from literal water to “living water” and culminates in a revelation about true worship.

translation.text: A woman from Samaria came to draw water. Jesus said to her, ‘Give me a drink.’ The Samaritan woman said, ‘How is it that you, a Jew, ask for a drink from me, a woman of Samaria?’ Jesus answered, ‘If you knew the gift of God, you would have asked him, and he would have given you living water.’
translation.original_text: ἔρχεται γυνὴ ἐκ τῆς Σαμαρείας ἀντλῆσαι ὕδωρ

translation.text: But the hour is coming, and is now here, when the true worshipers will worship the Father in spirit and truth, for the Father is seeking such people to worship him. God is spirit, and those who worship him must worship in spirit and truth.
translation.original_text: ἀλλὰ ἔρχεται ὥρα καὶ νῦν ἐστιν ὅτε οἱ ἀληθινοὶ προσκυνηταὶ προσκυνήσουσιν τῷ πατρὶ ἐν πνεύματι καὶ ἀληθείᾳ

translator_notes.note: The declaration “God is spirit” (πνεῦμα ὁ θεός) is one of the few ontological statements about God’s nature in the New Testament. It was central to later debates about divine incorporeality and the possibility of theosis.
